examples.title: Node Structure Definition
examples.description: Defines a self-referential structure for linked list nodes
examples.explanation: Contains integer data field and pointer to next node of same type. The 'next' field enables dynamic linking between nodes.

examples.title: Algorithm: Insert Node at Head
examples.description: Adds new node to beginning of linked list
examples.code:
ALGORITHM InsertAtHead
BEGIN
 CREATE newNode
 newNode.data ← value
 newNode.next ← head
 head ← newNode
END
examples.explanation:
1. Allocate memory for new node
2. Initialize data field
3. Point new node to current head
4. Update head pointer to new node

examples.title: Insert Node Implementation
examples.description: C function to insert node at list beginning
examples.explanation: Uses double pointer to modify head pointer. Demonstrates dynamic memory allocation using malloc().

examples.title: Algorithm: Traverse List
examples.description: Print all elements in linked list
examples.code:
ALGORITHM TraverseList
BEGIN
 current ← head
 WHILE current ≠ NULL DO
 PRINT current.data
 current ← current.next
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Uses temporary pointer to iterate through list. Terminates when NULL pointer (end of list) is reached.

examples.title: Algorithm: Concatenate Two Lists
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Concatenate
BEGIN
 IF list1 = NULL THEN
 list1 ← list2
 ELSE
 current ← list1
 WHILE current.next ≠ NULL DO
 current ← current.next
 END WHILE
 current.next ← list2
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: Handles empty first list case. Finds last node using traversal, then links to second list.
